steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify both triangles as right isosceles triangles
steps.content:
In triangle PQR with P(2,3), Q(2,6), R(5,3):
Q is directly above P (x = 2) and R is directly right of P (y = 3), so sides PQ and PR are perpendicular. Thus angle P = 90 degrees.

The distances are |PQ| = 3 and |PR| = 3, so PQ = PR, making PQR an isosceles right triangle.

Similarly, in triangle LMN with L(2,3), M(2,6+k), N(5+k,3):
M is directly above L and N is directly right of L, so angle L = 90 degrees.

The distances are |LM| = 3+k and |LN| = 3+k, so LM = LN, making LMN an isosceles right triangle.

steps.title: Apply the 45-45-90 triangle property
steps.content:
In an isosceles right triangle, the right angle is 90 degrees and both other angles must be 45 degrees (since they are equal and sum to 90 degrees).

Therefore:
  • In triangle PQR: angle Q = 45 degrees, so t = 45 degrees
  • In triangle LMN: angle N = 45 degrees

steps.title: Express angle N in terms of t
steps.content:
Since angle N = 45 degrees and t = 45 degrees, we have:
$$\angle N = 45^{\circ} = 90^{\circ} - 45^{\circ} = 90^{\circ} - t^{\circ}$$
That matches Choice C — $\left(90-t\right)^\circ$.
